$schema-comment: known-failure manifest — each entry documents an accepted-failing test with a reason and a categorization. The corpus runner classifies real fails against this manifest: matching entry → KNOWN-FAIL; missing entry → FAIL (real regression); entry exists but test now passes → UNEXPECTED-PASS (prompts cleanup); entry exists but test fails differently → KNOWN-FAIL-CHANGED (diagnostic alert).

$category-refs.deferred-fs: fs writable adapter deferred for external integration — see CLAUDE.md scope decision
$category-refs.deferred-net: net.createServer / TCP sockets deferred for external integration
$category-refs.deferred-child-process: child_process.spawn JSPI fix is scoped/ready but a multi-day session (task #177); infrastructure exists in host-worker.ts
$category-refs.deferred-vm-cross-realm: real cross-realm vm needs iframe-RPC or ShadowRealm; same-realm preset is best-effort fallback
$category-refs.inspector-not-supported: V8 inspector backend not exposed to browser page JS; see NOTES.md inspector-not-supported
$category-refs.v8-natives: tests using %PrepareFunctionForOptimization etc require --allow-natives-syntax in V8; not enabled in browser builds
$category-refs.v8-error-prepare-stack-trace: Error.prepareStackTrace + V8 CallSite API not implemented in our vm shim; test-assert-calltracker, test-util-getcallsites depend on it
$category-refs.asynclocalstorage-promise-hooks: AsyncLocalStorage promise-hook integration unsupported (universal browser-Node-runtime gap)
$category-refs.no-marker-bootstrap: test fails before driver emits PASS/FAIL marker; common.js loading issue likely tied to specific binding
$category-refs.drain-chain-depth-mustcall: mustCall verifier fires before deeply-chained async work settles; our env's per-poll-oneoff drain takes ~50ms which compounds across 5+ nextTick→emit chains. Watchdog gives 5s but some captureRejections chains exceed
$category-refs.corpus-driver-harness-uncaught: test expects uncaughtException handler to fire on a thrown sync error; our corpus driver's try/catch around require() swallows it. Real harness limitation, not a Node-compat gap. Would need driver to forward to test's uncaughtException listeners.
$category-refs.wasm-aliased-output-buffer: real bug: gunzipSync/inflateSync return the internal writeState typed-array bytes instead of decompressed output, same wasm-aliased-buffer family as brotli init-params (which we DID fix). Needs targeted preset; ~1-2h investigation.
